records.ety: [OE. rocke; akin to D. rok, rokken, G. rocken, OHG. roccho, Dan. rok, Icel. rokkr. Cf. Rocket a firework.]

defs: A distaff used in spinning; the staff or frame about which flax is arranged, and from which the thread is drawn in spinning.
quotes: Sad Clotho held the rocke, the whiles the thread By grisly Lachesis was spun with pain, That cruel Atropos eftsoon undid.

records.ety: [OF. roke, F. roche; cf. Armor. roc'h, and AS. rocc.]

defs: A large concreted mass of stony material; a large fixed stone or crag. See Stone.
quotes: Come one, come all! this rock shall fly From its firm base as soon as I.

defs: Any natural deposit forming a part of the earth's crust, whether consolidated or not, including sand, earth, clay, etc., when in natural beds.
That which resembles a rock in firmness; a defense; a support; a refuge.
quotes: The Lord is my rock, and my fortress.

defs: Fig.: Anything which causes a disaster or wreck resembling the wreck of a vessel upon a rock.
The striped bass. See under Bass.
notes: ☞ This word is frequently used in the formation of self-explaining compounds; as, rock-bound, rock-built, rock-ribbed, rock-roofed, and the like.

records.ety: [AS. roccian; akin to Dan. rokke to move, to snake; cf. Icel. rukkja to pull, move, G. rücken to move, push, pull.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Rocked (?);p. pr. & vb. n. Rocking.]

defs: To cause to sway backward and forward, as a body resting on a support beneath; as, to rock a cradle or chair; to cause to vibrate; to cause to reel or totter.
quotes: A rising earthquake rocked the ground.

defs: To move as in a cradle; hence, to put to sleep by rocking; to still; to quiet.
notes: ☞ Rock differs from shake, as denoting a slower, less violent, and more uniform motion, or larger movements. It differs from swing, which expresses a vibratory motion of something suspended.

defs: To move or be moved backward and forward; to be violently agitated; to reel; to totter.
quotes: The rocking town Supplants their footsteps.

defs: To roll or saway backward and forward upon a support; as, to rock in a rocking-chair.
